sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see something important. The token split into two pieces:
- id=6: N (green), 5 pixels, [30,24]-[32,25] - left part of the token
- id=7: W (white), 4 pixels, [30,25]-[32,26] - right part of the token
The original 3x3 token (9 pixels) with a white center has split. The green border (8 pixels minus center) is now 5 pixels, and the white center area is 4 pixels. This suggests the token is being "pulled apart" as it moves.
Wait, actually looking more carefully:
- id=6: N, 5 px, [30,24]-[32,25] - that's a 3x2 area (rows 30-32, cols 24-25) = 6 cells but only 5 are N
- id=7: W, 4 px, [30,25]-[32,26] - that's a 3x2 area (rows 30-32, cols 25-26) = 6 cells but only 4 are W
So the token seems to have been split or transformed. The white center expanded to the right side. This is interesting - it seems like the token is being "stretched" or "transformed" as it moves.
Let me look at the ASCII more carefully to understand the exact pattern.

</parameter>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the current state:
```
R30: wwwwwwwwwNNWNNNwwwpp
R31: wwwwwwwwwNWWNBNwwwpp
R32: cwwwwwwwwNNWNNNwwwpp
```
So the left token (originally at cols 18-20) has moved to cols 24-26 but it's been split:
- Cols 24-25: NN/N/NN (green)
- Col 25-26: W/W/W (white) - but wait, col 25 has both N and W
Let me re-read:
R30: ...NNWNNN...
R31: ...NWWNBN...
R32: ...NNWNNN...
So at cols 24-29:
- Col 24: N/N/N (green)
- Col 25: N/W/N (green/white/green)
- Col 26: W/W/W (white)
- Col 27: N/N/N (green - second token)
- Col 28: B/B/B (black center of second token)
- Col 29: N/N/N (green - second token)
Wait, that doesn't add up. Let me count more carefully.
The string is "wwwwwwwwwNNWNNNwwwpp"
Starting from col 15:
- 15-23: wwwwwwwww (9 w's)
- 24: N
- 25: N
- 26: W
- 27: N
- 28: N
- 29: N
- 30-32: www
- 33-34: pp
So the layout is:
- Cols 24-25: NN (green, part of first token)
- Col 26: W (white, part of first token's center that expanded)
- Cols 27-29: NNN (second token)
- Cols 30-32: www (white square)
But the segmentation says:
- id=6: N, 5 px, [30,24]-[32,25] - left green part
- id=7: W, 4 px, [30,25]-[32,26] - white part
- id=8: N, 8 px, [30,27]-[32,29] - second token
So the first token (originally 3x3 with white center) has split into:
- Green border: 5 pixels (cols 24-25, rows 30-32)
- White center: 4 pixels (cols 25-26, rows 30-32)
This is a strange transformation. It seems like the token is being "stretched" as it moves right, with the white center expanding to the right.
Let me think about what the game might be. Maybe the tokens need to be moved into the containers, and the movement mechanic causes them to stretch or transform. Or maybe the goal is to merge the two tokens somehow.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. The first token had a WHITE center (W), and the second has a BLACK center (B). When the first token moved right, its white center expanded. Maybe the game is about moving tokens to fill the containers?
Let me try pressing LEFT to undo the movement and see if the token goes back to normal.
